name: Trepucó
description: A Talayotic settlement (UNESCO) with one of the largest and best-preserved taulas on the island (4.8 m high), set within a nineteenth-century fortified enclosure. Close to Maó, it makes an ideal first encounter with the island's prehistory.

highlights: one of the largest taulas on the island (4.8 m), very close to Maó
the fortified Talayotic settlement (UNESCO World Heritage)
a short visit to Talayotic culture without leaving the capital
